heroHeadline: PLA Surges to 17 Sorties on April 11 — Highest Since March 29 — Day After Xi-KMT Summit; 15/17 Cross Median Line; Cheng Beijing Press Conference; KMT Defense Budget Boycott Continues
heroSubtitle: PLA surged to 17 aircraft sorties on April 11 (15 of 17 crossing the median line into northern + SW ADIZ) — highest since the March 29 peak of 19 — the day after Xi Jinping met KMT Chair Cheng Li-wun at the Great Hall of the People (first CPC-KMT leader summit in ~10 years); PLA post-summit surge confirms dual-track pressure-and-dialogue strategy; Cheng held Beijing press conference Apr 11 defending Xi meeting as 'deterrence through dialogue'; KMT lawmakers continue boycotting cross-caucus negotiations on Taiwan's NT$1.25T special defense budget; 33 days to Trump-Xi summit (May 14-15); Foreign Policy: 'Taiwan's Budget Crisis Gives China an Opening'
footerNote: Data sourced from Taiwan MND, US DoD, DSCA, CSIS ChinaPower, Global Taiwan Institute, AIT, Reuters, AP, Bloomberg, NPR, Taipei Times, Focus Taiwan, Nikkei Asia, Japan Times, ANI News, Tribune India, Vision Times, South China Morning Post, Taiwan News, Asia Times, The Asia Cable, GlobalSecurity.org, Xinhua, CGTN, The Diplomat, Washington Times, Global Times, People's Daily Online, Al Jazeera, The Manila Times, and institutional analyses. All claims reflect multi-pole sourcing across Western, East Asian, and international media.
